summary: Completed end-to-end backend testing for AgendaPro (37 tests, 100% pass). Covered auth (register/login/me/role-based), services CRUD + filters, availability + slot calculator (past dates, invalid dates, busy-slot exclusion, duration fit), appointments full lifecycle (create, confirm, reschedule, cancel by customer/provider, complete), reviews (one per appointment, completed only, provider blocked), and provider stats (revenue/rating/counts). Found and FIXED one critical bug in POST /api/auth/register: ObjectId serialization error (500) caused by not popping Mongo `_id` after insert_one (doc mutated). Login already handled this; register did not.

minor.issue: Sender email 'noreply@agendapro.com' is not verified in SendGrid; every background email call logs 'SendGrid error ... 403 Forbidden'. Flow is non-blocking (by design). Main agent should verify sender OR document this as expected in production.
Endpoint deletes cookie but client-stored Bearer token remains valid until JWT expiry (7 days). Consider a token blacklist if logout must be strict.

Busy-slot detection only looks at pending/confirmed appts. A newly created `rescheduling` status appointment (temporary state during reschedule) is NOT included in busy list; low risk because reschedule immediately moves out of it, but on an error path where status stays `rescheduling`, its original slot becomes bookable by others AND by rescheduling itself. Consider including `rescheduling` in busy filter.

Backend .env uses CORS_ORIGINS='*' while cookies are set with samesite=none + secure=true which requires credentials + explicit origin. Browsers will reject credentialed requests with wildcard origin. Frontend currently uses Bearer token fallback so it works, but cookie-based auth via browser will not.

action_items: VERIFIED FIX APPLIED: /app/backend/server.py register endpoint now pops _id before returning user (previously caused 500 on /api/auth/register).
Validate/verify SendGrid sender identity for noreply@agendapro.com OR set a verified sender in SENDER_EMAIL, otherwise all notification emails silently fail.
Consider tightening CORS_ORIGINS to explicit frontend origin(s) so cookie-based auth works in browsers (currently relies on Bearer token fallback).
Optionally include 'rescheduling' status in the slot-busy filter to guarantee temporary reschedule state still blocks other bookings.
critical_code_review_comments: ObjectId leak was present in register (fixed). Ensure every insert_one caller drops _id from return payload — POST /services, /appointments, /reviews already do this; please audit any future endpoints.
server.py is ~812 lines; consider splitting into routers (auth, services, availability, appointments, reviews, stats) once feature set grows.

`get_slots` is invoked internally from create_appointment / reschedule using `provider_id=..., service_id=..., date=...` as kwargs. Works today because Query(...) defaults allow kwargs, but coupling controllers to route handlers is fragile — prefer a private helper.
No rate-limit or brute-force protection on /auth/login. Out of scope for MVP but flag for production.
JWT_SECRET is committed in .env; acceptable for preview but must be rotated for production.
Email HTML uses f-string concatenation with user-controlled fields (names). Low XSS risk (email client sandbox), but HTML-escape names to be safe.
test_credentials: Ephemeral TEST_* accounts are created per-session via /api/auth/register using the fixtures in conftest.py. No static credentials required.
seed_data_creation: None permanent. Each pytest session registers fresh TEST_customer_* / TEST_provider_* users, 1 service, 7 availability windows, 1 appointment, 1 review. Service is deleted at the end; users/appointments/reviews remain but are uniquely suffixed and safe to ignore.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_agendapro.py. Run with `python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from env. Tests are order-dependent within classes via pytest.<attr> stash (service_id, appt_id, review_id) — do not parallelize with xdist. To cover frontend, register fresh users or reuse suggested credentials in /app/memory/test_credentials.md (create them via API first).
rca of the issue: RCA of 500 on /api/auth/register: Mongo's insert_one mutates the inserted dict in-place, appending the generated `_id` (ObjectId). The register handler built `user_public` from the mutated doc without dropping `_id`. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize bson.ObjectId → TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable. Login handler already called `user.pop('_id', None)`; register missed it. Fix: added `doc.pop('_id', None)` before building user_public. Reproduction: POST /api/auth/register with any valid body → 500. After fix: 200 with sanitized user object.
